data.title: Oil rises as Putin continues to say 'nyet' to Ukraine peace deal
data.description:
Crude oil futures rose Wednesday after high-level talks between the U.S. and Russia failed to yield any significant progress in ending the war in Ukraine, raising the prospect of lasting sanctions on Russian oil, which could limit any increase in global oil supplies.

The lack of a breakthrough in peace talks, coupled with Ukraine's persistent attacks on Russian energy infrastructure, have been driving prices modestly higher, and Russia's President Putin has threatened retaliatory measures on vessels from nations helping Ukraine, but gains are capped by concerns over softening global demand and rising supply.

The latest Energy Information Administration inventory report showed U.S. crude stocks rising by 600,000 barrels to 427.5 million barrels in the week ended November 28, compared with analyst expectations in a Reuters poll for an 821,000-barrel draw.

The EIA also said U.S. gasoline inventories rose by a larger than expected 4.5 million barrels, the most in any week since May, to 214.42 million barrels, and distillate stocks, which include diesel and heating oil, rose by 2.1 million barrels to 114.3 million barrels.

Meanwhile, the U.S. Treasury Department imposed sanctions on key affiliates of the Venezuelan gang known as the Tren de Aragua, saying the network’s drug trafficking and human smuggling operations are a grave threat.

"Overall global supply still remains pretty ample. The market is adjusting itself, as the Ukraine-Russian peace agreement is going to be delayed," BOK Financial's Dennis Kissler said in a note. "The market is still in a very nervous trade because we've got major geopolitical issues."

Front-month Nymex crude (CL1:COM) for January delivery closed +0.5% to $58.95/bbl, and front-month Brent crude (CO1:COM) for February delivery finished +0.3% to $62.67/bbl.

U.S. natural gas futures (NG1:COM) for January delivery traded above $5/MMBtu for the first time since December 2022, settling +3.2% to $4.995/MMBtu.

ETFs: (USO), (BNO), (UCO), (SCO), (USL), (DBO), (DRIP), (GUSH), (USOI), (UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG), (XLE)

data.title: C3.ai outlines $72M–$80M Q3 revenue target while expanding federal and enterprise AI partnerships
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: C3.ai (AI) Q2 2026

Management View

  • CEO Stephen Ehikian stated that "our results in Q2 were solid. Revenue grew 7% sequentially and bookings increased by 49% sequentially to $86 million." He highlighted strong high-value deal activity with "17 agreements over $1 million and 6 agreements over $5 million." The CEO acknowledged the extended 43-day government shutdown created headwinds, particularly affecting the Federal business, shipbuilding, health care, manufacturing, and industrials, but noted "we delivered a fine quarter, and I'm proud of the company's execution."
  • The company saw "significant traction in the Federal business," with total bookings across Federal, Defense, and Aerospace increasing by 89% year-over-year and representing 45% of total bookings. New and expanded agreements were signed with several U.S. government agencies, including the Department of Health and Human Services, Department of War, Intelligence Community, Army, and others.
  • The launch of C3 AI Agentic Process Automation was announced, which "materially changes how enterprises will run their operations and expand the scope of what customers can accomplish with our platform." This innovation aims to serve the robotic process automation market with AI software agents.
  • The CEO emphasized a sharpened focus on sales execution, stating, "I am raising the bar of execution with sharper qualification and rigorous deal reviews" and is prioritizing expansions in strategic lighthouse accounts, focusing on sectors such as federal, energy, health care, manufacturing, and select commercial markets.
  • CFO Hitesh Lath stated, "Total revenue for the quarter was $75.1 million, a quarter-over-quarter increase of 7%. Subscription revenue for the quarter was $70.2 million, a quarter-over-quarter increase of 16.5% and representing 93% of total revenue."

Outlook

  • CFO Hitesh Lath provided guidance: "Our revenue guidance for Q3 of fiscal year '26 is $72 million to $80 million. Our revenue guidance for fiscal year '26 is $289.5 million to $309.5 million."
  • The company expects "sequentially higher sales and marketing expenses in Q3 and Q4 due to major marketing events, including World Economic Forum and Transform."
  • Management stated that "the execution of this plan will facilitate our return to growth and provide a clear pathway to cash generation and non-GAAP profitability."

Financial Results

  • CFO Hitesh Lath reported, "Total revenue for the quarter was $75.1 million." Subscription revenue was $70.2 million, and revenue from software licenses not requiring maintenance and support services was $21.9 million. Professional Services revenue was $4.9 million.
  • Bookings during the quarter reached $86.4 million, an increase of 49% from last quarter.
  • Non-GAAP gross profit for the quarter was $40.9 million, with a non-GAAP gross margin of 54%. Professional Services gross margin reached 72%.
  • Non-GAAP operating loss for the quarter was $42.2 million, and non-GAAP net loss was $34.8 million, or ($0.25) per share. Free cash flow for the quarter was negative $46.9 million. The company ended the quarter with $675 million in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities.
  • The company signed 20 IPDs, including 6 gen AI IPDs, and had 269 active IPDs at quarter’s end.

Q&A

  • Patrick Walravens, Citizens JMP Securities: Asked CEO Ehikian to explain the business decline and steps to recovery. CEO Ehikian responded, "The biggest thing I would say is sales execution...fell off. It was totally unacceptable," but emphasized, "the demand for C3 and enterprise AI is only accelerating," and highlighted the plan and operational rigor now in place to deliver results.
  • Walravens followed up on confidence in returning to growth and profitability. CEO Ehikian replied, "Q2 execution was very strong. It was solid results. I'm confident in the opportunity ahead of us. We got to execute, Pat."
  • Matthew Calitri, Needham & Company: Clarified revenue mix and outlook for Professional Services. CFO Lath said, "in the long term, we would expect ProServ mix to continue to stay between 10% to 20%. Our Professional Services mix this quarter was on the lower side...primarily due to lower PES revenue."
  • Calitri asked about public sector outlook. CEO Ehikian stated federal strength "is going to be a durable growth engine for C3" and identified multiple long-term positive trends.
  • Brian Essex, JPMorgan: Inquired about management accountability and metrics. CEO Ehikian emphasized, "a big driver of our growth is going to be the IPD motion," and pointed to bookings and growth in IPD and Production revenue as leading indicators.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts focused on business decline, execution issues, and the path to recovery, with a neutral to slightly negative tone, seeking clarity on sales execution and confidence in growth.
  • Management maintained a confident and positive tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing operational rigor and opportunity size. In Q&A, CEO Ehikian acknowledged past shortcomings but stressed improved discipline and a strong team, stating, "I'm confident in the opportunity ahead of us." Compared to the previous quarter, sentiment improved from defensive and apologetic to constructive and forward-looking.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter provided more detailed execution plans and clear operational objectives, with new accountability measures for management and employees.
  • Guidance for Q3 and the full year was reinstated, compared to the previous quarter’s withdrawal of guidance.
  • Bookings and revenue grew sequentially, and operational losses narrowed from the previous quarter.
  • Management tone shifted from defensive and apologetic to confident and focused on execution. Analyst questioning remained focused on execution and recovery but was less negative than in the previous quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • CEO Ehikian cited the prolonged government shutdown as a significant headwind, particularly in the Federal segment, with lingering impacts across sectors.
  • Management acknowledged ongoing challenges in sales execution and highlighted the need for continued rigorous evaluation and delivery of value to customers.
  • CFO Lath pointed to moderated gross margins due to high mix of IPDs and investments in support capacity as factors affecting near-term profitability.

Final Takeaway

C3.ai’s management underscored a return to disciplined execution and operational rigor, supported by strengthened partnerships and a sharpened focus on high-potential sectors. The company’s detailed financial and operational planning, coupled with sequential growth in revenue and bookings, forms the basis for renewed guidance and a pathway to profitability, with leadership committed to transparent performance measurement and accountability across all business units.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: SideChannel signals 2026 expansion with Enclave as primary growth engine, backed by new leadership hires
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: SideChannel, Inc. (SDCH) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Brian Haugli, CEO & Director, highlighted that "Fiscal 2025 was a year of disciplined focus and strategic investment for SideChannel. While revenue remained relatively flat at approximately $7.4 million, we maintained a strong gross margin near 48% and held operating expenses in check." He emphasized that investments were made to position the company for "meaningful expansion in 2026, particularly around Enclave."
  • Haugli stated, "Our focus shifts decisively from foundation building to scaling adoption" for Enclave, naming it the "cornerstone of that long-term growth strategy." He outlined three demand areas: "zero trust segmentation, secure remote access without VPN dependency, and...protection of high-value assets across hybrid and OT or operational technology environments."
  • Two key leadership hires were announced: Jamie Wolf as Chief Marketing Officer, focused on strengthening market presence, and Rick Dill as enterprise Account Executive for Enclave, tasked with accelerating large deployments.
  • Haugli described a shift "from infrastructure building to revenue acceleration" and reiterated SideChannel’s commitment to "strong financial discipline." He expressed confidence entering fiscal 2026 with a "stronger commercial and government Enclave pipeline, an expanded leadership team fully aligned to growth execution, a refined go-to-market strategy centered on zero trust, microsegmentation and vCISO-led cybersecurity programs."
  • Haugli concluded, "Fiscal 2025 was a year of strategic positioning. Fiscal 2026 is a year of execution and expansion."
  • Ryan Polk, CFO & Secretary, confirmed, "We don't -- we didn't disclose and haven't disclosed revenue by quarter and by category...there won't be a Q4 results in there. So I can't give you those specific numbers in this call. We won't be disclosing that information."

Outlook

  • Haugli projected, "We believe 2026 will be a breakout year for Enclave revenue growth, driven by stronger channel execution, direct enterprise sales and increased awareness of microsegmentation as a practical zero trust control."
  • He stated that every investment is measured "against long-term shareholder value" and the company enters 2026 with "a growing demand for practical alternatives to legacy perimeter security."
  • Haugli identified the "biggest risk going into FY 2026" as "execution velocity, ensuring that increased market interest converts into closed revenue at scale. That's precisely why we invested in leadership across marketing and enterprise sales."

Financial Results

  • Haugli reported that "revenue remained relatively flat at approximately $7.4 million" with a "strong gross margin near 48% and held operating expenses in check."
  • No revenue or margin breakdown by product or service was disclosed for Q4 or the full year.
  • The company maintained a zero-debt position and expressed satisfaction with its balance sheet structure, with no plans to raise capital or debt before April-May 2026, contingent on performance in Q1 and Q2.

Q&A

  • Luke Wheatley, private investor, asked about plans for raising capital or debt after warrants expire in early 2026. Haugli responded, "Right now, we are just -- really just kind of looking at April as a milestone...there have been no discussions or plans for raising any capital or taking on any debt. We're still very happy of our balance sheet structure, maintaining zero debt."
  • Wheatley also inquired about the revenue split between vCISO and Enclave in Q4. Polk reiterated, "We didn't disclose and haven't disclosed revenue by quarter and by category...we won't be disclosing that information."
  • A question was posed via email regarding Enclave's applicability for drones. Haugli explained that Enclave "can be deployed to these types of devices," especially in military and DoD contexts, and outlined ongoing efforts with federal sales partners.
  • Haugli summarized board questions, emphasizing that "Enclave is absolutely central to our long-term strategy. Our vCISO and advisory services remain important demand drivers...but Enclave is our primary growth engine."
  • On risks for FY 2026, Haugli cited "execution velocity" and addressed market forces and client retention strategies, noting the need to convert demand into scale revenue.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst tone was inquisitive but not confrontational, with focus on capital strategy, revenue composition, and product applicability. No negative or pressing sentiment was apparent.
  • Management maintained a confident and strategic tone, with Haugli asserting, "We are confident in our market opportunity, our leadership team, our technology and our ability to deliver increasing value for our customers and our shareholders."
  • Compared to Q3, management's tone shifted from cautious optimism about product traction to focused confidence in execution and growth, particularly around Enclave.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The company moved from emphasizing foundational investments and expanding Enclave’s initial footprint in Q3 to prioritizing "scaling adoption" and "revenue acceleration" in Q4.
  • New leadership hires in marketing and enterprise sales represent a shift from infrastructure building to execution.
  • Discussions about capital needs remained consistent, with continued preference for zero debt and no immediate capital raise plans.
  • Management’s confidence level increased, as seen in Haugli’s statements about "breakout year" potential and Enclave’s central role in strategy.
  • Analyst questions in both quarters focused on transparency around contracts, revenue composition, and new product adoption, but Q4 saw more emphasis on execution risk and growth scalability.

Risks and Concerns

  • Haugli identified "execution velocity" as the primary risk, specifically "ensuring that increased market interest converts into closed revenue at scale."
  • Management highlighted industry budget constraints and changing customer buying patterns as ongoing market challenges.
  • No additional financial or operational risks were disclosed.

Final Takeaway

SideChannel’s management underscored a transition from strategic positioning in 2025 to execution and expansion in 2026, with Enclave positioned as the primary growth engine. New leadership hires in marketing and enterprise sales are intended to drive pipeline growth and large-scale deployments. Maintaining zero debt and financial discipline remains a core priority, while the company’s outlook for 2026 centers on capitalizing on increased market demand for zero trust solutions and converting pipeline momentum into scalable revenue growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Crescent Energy sells non-operated DJ Basin assets in $90M deal
data.description:
Crescent Energy (CRGY) said post-market Wednesday it agreed to sell its non-operated DJ Basin assets to an unnamed private buyer for $90 million in cash, in the latest update to the company's previously announced non-core divestiture program.

Including the latest assets, whose current production amounts to ~7,000 boe/day, Crescent (CRGY) said it has executed agreements exceeding $900 million so far this year, after closing previously announced conventional Rockies and Barnett divestitures, and the company expects to close the remainder of its announced non-core asset sales before year-end.

Crescent (CRGY) said proceeds from the transactions will be used to reduce outstanding borrowings on its revolving credit facility.

data.title: MDU Resources launches $200M stock offering with forward component
data.description:
MDU Resources (MDU) -2.8% post-market Wednesday after saying it commenced a public offering of $200 million of common shares, with an underwriters option to purchase up to $30 million of additional common shares.

In connection with the offering, MDU Resources (MDU) said it plans to enter into separate forward sale agreements with various banks in which the company will agree to sell to the forward purchasers ~$200 million of common shares at an initial forward sale price equal to the price at which the underwriters purchase the shares in the offering.

MDU Resources (MDU) will not initially receive any proceeds from the sale of shares by the forward sellers to the underwriters; if the company elects physical settlement of the forward sale agreements, it plans to use any net proceeds for general corporate purposes, including paying down debt, capital expenditures, and acquisitions, including payment in 2026 for a 49% stake in the Badger Wind Farm project.

data.title: Crown Point appoints Brian Moss as interim President and CEO
data.description:
• Crown Point Energy (CWV:CA) on Wednesday said its board of directors has appointed Brian Moss as the interim President and CEO of the company.
  • Moss has been a director of Crown Point since December 2017 and also served as a director from May 2012 to April 2015. In addition, he served as Crown Point's President and CEO from November 2016 to February 2022. 
  • Crown Point also announced that Marisa Tormakh has resigned as the company's Vice-President, Finance and CFO to pursue other opportunities. 
  • The Board said it has appointed Marcos Esteves to replace Tormakh as Vice-President, Finance and CFO.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Volatile silver could spike to $75 or fall to $40, Bloomberg’s McGlone says
data.description:
Silver's remarkable rally that has sent prices doubling this year is “a little scary,” Bloomberg Intelligence senior strategist Mike McGlone said Wednesday, seeing the potential for a surge to $75/oz or dropping back to $40/oz because of the metal's volatility.

Silver has a history of extreme rallies - prices currently are 83% above their five-year average - that, historically, have never ended well, McGlone said.

"The 1980 peak at ~$49.50/oz was not surpassed until 2011, and 1993 marked the low ~$3.60, about a 93% drawdown. A comparable stretch occurred in 1974, when year-end silver closed at $4.47, a level not exceeded again until 1977, marking a relatively mild time correction," McGlone said. "The 2010 rally to close at ~$31 was the most recent extended example: $49.80 marked the 2011 high and $11.64 was the nadir in 2020."

With annual market volatility of ~30%, McGlone cautioned that a move of one standard deviation in price could push silver to as high as $75/oz or as low as $40/oz next year.

Silver slipped lower on Wednesday for a second straight session while gold rose modestly after weaker U.S. private payrolls data showing a loss of 32,000 jobs in November reinforced expectations of a December interest rate cut by the Federal Reserve.

"The Fed is still likely to accompany a further cut next week with more hawkish messaging about the prospect for future loosening," Stephen Brown of Capital Economics said in a note, as CME's Fed Watch tool now shows an 89% chance of a Fed rate cut.

Front-month Comex (XAUUSD:CUR) gold for December delivery closed +0.3% to $4,199.30/oz, its seventh gain in the past eight sessions, and front-month Comex December silver (XAGUSD:CUR) ended -0.1% to $57.921/oz.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Former FDA commissioners castigate plan to change vaccine approval standards
data.description:
A dozen former FDA commissioners have come together to pen a scathing op-ed opposing a plan recently unveiled by the agency's top vaccine official, Vinay Prasad, to drastically change the standards used to approve shots.

Prasad's email to staff at the FDA's Center for Biologics Evaluation and Research on Nov. 28 shocked many public health and infectious diseases experts who saw the email as an indication that the administration is taking a tougher stand on vaccine approvals. Prasad stated that the changes were needed due to the deaths of at least 10 children that he claims were caused by the COVID-19 jab.

"The existing regulatory model builds public trust by encouraging open information exchange and rigorous, transparent scientific debate," the perspective piece in The New England Journal of Medicine reads. The measures outlined in the memo "are the latest in a series of troubling changes at the FDA, including substantial departures of FDA staff, that could diminish both the FDA’s strength and Americans’ health and safety."

"The proposed guidelines would dramatically change vaccine regulation on the basis of a reinterpretation of selective evidence and by a process that breaks sharply with the norms that have anchored the FDA’s globally respected scientific integrity. If enacted, the framework would impede the ability to update vaccines to keep up with the natural evolution of respiratory viruses or changes in the prevalence of bacterial serotypes; it would also suppress innovation and competition," the former commissioners warn.

The op-ed also argues that rather than enhance vaccine science, vaccines will now face "a substantially higher and more subjective approval bar. The proposed measures will slow the replacement of older products with better ones and will create potentially prohibitive expenses for new market entrants, especially small biotechnology companies." 

Prasad's memo also calls for new studies for vaccines that are regularly updated, such as flu and COVID. But doing this, the commissioners say, "would delay the arrival of better-matched vaccines when new outbreaks emerge or when additional groups of patients could benefit."

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Nasdaq U.S. matched equity volume falls M/M, rises Y/Y in November
data.description:
Nasdaq's (NDAQ) U.S. matched equity volume slid to 53.4M shares in November from 65.6M in October, but advanced from 41.1M a year ago, it said on Wednesday.

U.S. equity options volume was 351M contracts last month, down from 423M in October and up from 304M in November 2024.

European equity volume of $78.0B in November, vs. $85.3B in the prior month and $67.2B in last year's November.

European options and futures volume of 4.3M contracts compares with 5.8M in October and 4.6M a year before.
